socket

Creates a socket that is bound to a specific service provider.

SOCKET socket(
    int af,
    int type,
    int protocol
);

Parameters

af
[in] Address family specification. Must be AF_INET.
type
[in] Type specification for the new socket.
Type Explanation
SOCK_STREAM Provides sequenced, reliable, two-way, connection-based byte streams. Uses TCP as the protocol.
SOCK_DGRAM Supports datagrams, which are connectionless, unreliable buffers of a fixed (typically small) maximum length. Uses UDP or VDP as the protocol.
protocol
[in] Protocol to be used with the socket.
Protocol Explanation
IPPROTO_TCP Use with SOCK_STREAM. Provides sequenced, reliable, two-way, connection-based byte streams.
IPPROTO_UDP Use with SOCK_DGRAM. Supports datagrams, which are connectionless, unreliable buffers of a fixed (typically small) maximum length.
IPPROTO_VDP Use with SOCK_DGRAM. Similar to IPPROTO_UDP. Use this protocol to send encrypted game data and unencrypted voice data in the same packet. See Winsock Implementation Notes for more information.

Return Values

If no error occurs, socket returns a descriptor referencing the new socket. Otherwise, a value of INVALID_SOCKET is returned, and a specific error code can be retrieved by calling WSAGetLastError.

Error code Meaning
WSANOTINITIALISED A successful WSAStartup call must occur before using this function.
WSAENETDOWN The network subsystem or the associated service provider has failed.
WSAEAFNOSUPPORT The specified address family is not supported.
WSAENOBUFS No buffer space is available. The socket cannot be created.
WSAEPROTONOSUPPORT The specified protocol is not supported, or does not match the socket type requested.

Remarks

The socket function causes a socket descriptor and any related resources to be allocated and bound to a specific transport-service provider. The socket that is created will have the overlapped attribute set.

All functions that allow overlapped operation (WSASend, WSARecv,WSASendTo, and WSARecvFrom) also support nonoverlapped usage on an overlapped socket if the values for parameters related to overlapped operation are NULL.

Connection-oriented sockets such as SOCK_STREAM provide full-duplex connections, and must be in a connected state before any data can be sent or received on it. A connection to another socket is created with a connect call. Once connected, data can be transferred using send and recv calls. When a session has been completed, a closesocket must be performed.

The communications protocols used to implement a reliable, connection-oriented socket ensure that data is not lost or duplicated. If data for which the peer protocol has buffer space cannot be successfully transmitted within a reasonable length of time, the connection is considered broken and subsequent calls will fail with the error code set to WSAETIMEDOUT.

Connectionless, message-oriented sockets allow sending and receiving of datagrams to and from arbitrary peers using sendto and recvfrom. If such a socket is connected to a specific peer, datagrams can be sent to that peer using send and can be received only from this peer using recv.

Requirements

Header: Declared in winsockx.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xnet.lib for titles that only support system link play, XOnline.lib for titles that support online play. Use the secure versions of the library file (Xnets.lib or XOnlines.lib) for final testing.

See Also

Winsock Overview, Winsock Functions | accept, bind, connect, getsockname, getsockopt, ioctlsocket, listen, recv, recvfrom, select, send, sendto, setsockopt, shutdown
